name: Kingdom of Croatia (Habsburg)

colors.symbolism: the upper stripe; a historic Croatian colour
the lower stripe; a historic Croatian colour

description: Two equal horizontal stripes of red over white.
history: The red-and-white bicolour used by the Kingdom of Croatia within the Habsburg Monarchy, notably from 1852 to 1861, after the red-white-blue Croatian tricolour raised in the 1848 national revival was banned by the imperial government. The tricolour was restored in 1861 and became the direct ancestor of the modern Croatian flag.
